title: 10 best herbal tinctures for shortness of breath
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for shortness of breath are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants that have been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. These tinctures work by soothing and relaxing the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion, and improving oxygen flow to the lungs. Examples include Lobelia (Lobelia inflata) and Thyme (Thymus vulgaris), which have a calming effect on the respiratory system, and Elecampane (Inula helenium) and Marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis), which help to reduce inflammation and soothe the bronchial tubes. By using these tinctures, individuals suffering from shortness of breath can expect improved oxygen intake, reduced wheezing, and increased overall quality of life, allowing them to participate in daily activities with greater ease and confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The tincture's active ingredients, such as echinacea and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier for oxygen to pass through and alleviating respiratory distress. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further reducing symptoms of shortness of breath and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas coneflower contains antioxidant compounds that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.

Expectorant properties: Kansas coneflower contains mucilages that can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it an effective natural expectorant for relieving respiratory congestion and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and triterpenoids, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease respiratory congestion and improve breathing.
Fruits: The fruits contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried echinacea angustifolia roots and organic vodka for the tincture preparation process.
Combine 500g of dried echinacea angustifolia roots with 1000ml of organic vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks to allow for the herbal extraction process.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with shortness of breath because its potent extract, Adiantum majus, has a profound impact on respiratory health. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and relax the airways, reducing constriction and obstruction caused by allergies or chronic conditions. Additionally, maidenhair tree's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm, making it easier to breathe and promoting healthy lung function. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and COPD, offering relief from shortness of breath and improving overall respiratory well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which may contribute to improved breathing.

Vasodilation and blood circulation enhancement: Ginkgo's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation may help increase oxygen delivery to the body, alleviating shortness of breath caused by reduced lung function or cardiovascular issues.

Bronchodilatory effects: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo extracts can relax airway muscles, leading to improved airflow and reduced bronchial constriction, which may help alleviate respiratory symptoms like shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to relax and soothe respiratory tract muscles, alleviating shortness of breath symptoms.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce congestion and ease breathing difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator set at 105°f for 2 hours or until crisp.
Combine 10 parts dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 90% ethanol in a clean glass jar and steep for 14 days shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with shortness of breath because its natural menthol content relaxes the airways, allowing for easier breathing. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties reduce constriction in the bronchial tubes, opening up pathways for smooth airflow. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the respiratory system help to alleviate feelings of anxiety and tension that can exacerbate shortness of breath. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal peppermint tinctures provide natural relief from shortness of breath, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath, and help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds relax the muscles in the bronchial tubes, reducing spasms that can constrict the airways and make breathing difficult.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are rich in menthol and other compounds that help to relax bronchial spasms, relieving shortness of breath.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of peppermint contain volatile oils that can help to thin mucus and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a pesticide-free environment weighing approximately 500g.
Chop the harvested peppermint leaves into small pieces and combine with 700ml of high-proof vodka or ethanol.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar and allow it to steep at room temperature for 6-8 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at a consistent refrigerated temperature below 40°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains thymol, a natural expectorant that loosens and clears mucus from the lungs. This allows for easier breathing and reduces congestion, making it an effective remedy for bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the airways, further easing respiratory distress. By promoting healthy lung function, herbal thyme tinctures can provide natural relief from shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath by constricting air passages and reducing lung capacity.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat respiratory infections such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and sinusitis, which can cause shortness of breath by inflaming the lungs and airways.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have a rich concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the lungs and relieving respiratory distress.
Barks: The barks of the thyme plant contain flavonoids that help to dilate the airways, allowing for easier breathing and reduced shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, ensure they are free of contaminants, and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 20 grams of the cleaned thyme herbs with 200 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily to allow maceration.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing the herbs gently to extract all liquid.
Transfer the final tincture to amber glass bottles, label with ingredients and preparation date, and store in a cool dark place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with shortness of breath because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and improve lung function. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root help to ease bronchial constriction, allowing for easier breathing and increased oxygen intake. Additionally, the soothing properties of licorice tincture can calm irritated airways and mucous membranes, further alleviating shortness of breath and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, which help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

Mucilage production: Licorice root stimulates the production of mucilage, a protective substance that soothes and lubricates the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and constriction of airways, which can help alleviate shortness of breath.

Adrenal cortex support: Licorice root has been shown to support the function of the adrenal cortex, which produces cortisol, a hormone that helps regulate breathing and response to stress. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals with chronic respiratory conditions who may experience exacerbations due to stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm respiratory issues, making them an effective remedy for alleviating shortness of breath.

Rhyzomes: Containing a higher concentration of bioactive compounds than the roots, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the airways and alleviate breathing difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reliable source according to traditional ayurvedic methods.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces to ensure maximum surface area for extraction.
Combine one part of chopped glycyrrhiza glabra roots with two parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and let it infuse for 14 days, shaking daily, allowing solvents to extract bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids, and label with preparation date and ingredient ratio.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains eucalyptus oil, which has natural decongestant and expectorant properties. When taken internally, the eucalyptus oil helps to clear mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, further alleviating shortness of breath symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath and respiratory distress.
Antispasmodic property: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture helps to relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can cause breathing difficulties.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the bark has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help combat respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum oil content.
Chop 500 grams of harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped eucalyptus globulus leaves with 1000 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and let it macerate for six weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the tincture from plant material after six weeks of maceration.
Transfer the clear eucalyptus globulus tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool place for future use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it is a natural bronchodilator, meaning it can help relax and expand airways in the lungs. The antioxidant properties in sage tincture also reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making breathing easier. Additionally, sage's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, further relieving shortness of breath symptoms. By addressing these underlying issues, sage tinctures can provide natural relief for individuals experiencing shortness of breath, promoting improved lung function and overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the body, which can exacerbate breathing difficulties.

Bronchodilatory effects: The volatile oils present in sage, particularly camphor and thujone, have been shown to dilate bronchial airways, making it easier to breathe and helping alleviate shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain a high concentration of volatile oils and saponins, which help to ease respiratory issues such as shortness of breath by reducing inflammation and opening up airways.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the sage plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory problems, including bronchitis and asthma, and are often used in tinctures for their expectorant properties that help to relieve congestion and shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when the dew is still present for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently brushing off any dirt or debris with a soft brush.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and transfer them to a clean glass jar leaving about an inch at the top.
Combine the chopped leaves with 60% proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts liquid allowing it to infuse for six weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and storing it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with shortness of breath because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the airways. The mucilage in mullein helps to reduce inflammation and irritation, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it an effective natural remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). By reducing inflammation and clearing out mucus, mullein tinctures can provide significant relief from shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Mullein helps relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can exacerbate shortness of breath.
Mucolytic properties: Mullein's saponins break down mucus, making it easier to expel and clearing the airways, which can help alleviate shortness of breath caused by congestion or excess mucus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein's flavonoids and phenolic acids reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, helping to soothe and calm irritated tissues, which can contribute to shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain mucilages that help soothe and calm respiratory issues, making them effective in treating shortness of breath.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate bronchial constriction and improve lung function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers for optimal potency.
Cut the harvested material into small pieces and combine it with a carrier liquid such as 80% ethanol or vodka.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a glass jar with a lid at room temperature for four weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place protected from light and heat and use within six months of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains a unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to ease respiratory issues. The bitter principles in yarrow, such as achillein and saponins, help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the airways, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and bronchial tubes, further alleviating shortness of breath. As a result, yarrow tinctures can provide relief for individuals suffering from chronic bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions characterized by shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling in the airways, which can constrict breathing passages and exacerbate shortness of breath.
Expectorant: Yarrow's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion that may be contributing to shortness of breath.

Vasodilator: Yarrow's vasodilating effects can help dilate blood vessels, improving oxygenation of the body and reducing strain on the respiratory system, which can help alleviate shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are known for their ability to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the lungs.

Flowers: The flowers of yarrow have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including shortness of breath, due to their expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, cutting at least 4 ounces for this recipe.
Wash the harvested material in cool water to remove any dirt or debris then gently shake off excess moisture.
Combine the cleaned plant material with 80 proof vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of one part herbs to two parts liquid.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and let it steep for 4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, squeezing the solids gently to extract as much liquid as possible.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and congestion in the lungs. The bioactive compounds in hyssop, such as volatile oils and flavonoids, help to relax the airways, making it easier to breathe deeply and efficiently. Additionally, hyssop's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further alleviating respiratory distress. This natural remedy can provide relief for those experiencing chronic bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions that cause shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Hyssop tincture helps relax bronchial spasms, reducing constriction and opening up airways to improve breathing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving shortness of breath.
Expectorant properties: Hyssop tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, further improving airflow and alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots are rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to ease respiratory congestion and relax airways, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
Leaves: Hyssop leaves contain volatile oils that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making breathing easier.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hyssopus officinalis leaves in the early morning to ensure maximum potency.
Clean and chop the harvested leaves into small pieces to release their oils and active compounds.
Combine 250ml of high-proof vodka or rum with 100g of chopped hyssopus officinalis leaves in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow maceration.
Strain and decant the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat shortness of breath with herbal tinctures is to combine a blend of calming and expectorant herbs. Lobelia inflata (Lobelia) helps relax bronchial muscles, while Thyme (Thymus vulgaris) and Echinacea (Echinacea spp.) stimulate the lungs to expel mucus and promote healthy respiration. Take 10-20 drops of a tincture blend three times daily in water or tea, and consult with a healthcare practitioner for personalized guidance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shortness of breath that are treated with herbal tinctures are asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and bronchitis. Herbal remedies such as thyme, eucalyptus, and mullein can help soothe and relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction. Tinctures containing herbs like ginkgo biloba and passionflower may also improve lung function and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and panic attacks, which can exacerbate breathing difficulties.
